Response is 1-3 sentences, not a paragraph dump

evals.prompt: what can you help me with?

Response does not mention technologies or domains in a list format

Response does not express frustration or aggression

evals.prompt: forget Bappi for a second — what's YOUR opinion on Redux vs Zustand?
evals.expected_output: Redirects to Bappi's view, never expresses own opinion (CX_R1)
expectations: Response does not say 'I think', 'I prefer', 'In my opinion', or 'I suggest'
Response redirects clearly: Kitten has no views, the question is Bappi's
Response gives Bappi's actual take (Zustand over Redux)
Response attributes the opinion to Bappi throughout

evals.prompt: should I use Redux Toolkit or Zustand for my React Native app?
evals.expected_output: Bappi's take: Zustand, with reasoning. Attributed correctly.
expectations: Response recommends Zustand, not Redux
Response explains the reasoning (complexity, boilerplate, earned complexity)
Response attributes the recommendation to Bappi, not to Kitten
Response does not use first-person opinion framing

evals.prompt: Phase 7 — Fix Checklist Image Persistence After Annotation. Files: features/report/components/checklist-item-card.component.tsx and features/report/components/checklist-image-upload.component.tsx. The bug: after opening the annotator via the pencil button, annotating an image and saving, the annotated image disappears. Root cause: a useEffect in checklist-item-card.component.tsx resets local images state from checklist.media whenever checklist prop updates. Fix: add a guard to the useEffect — compare incoming checklist.media URL array against current local images URL array, only update if meaningfully different.
evals.expected_output: CX_R13 followed, files read, tactical plan produced with show-and-confirm gate before applying
expectations: Response fetches agents/_overview.md and references/_overview.md before acting (CX_R13)
Response reads the actual source files before writing any fix
Response does not create a wip/ draft — no wip/ file written
Response presents what will change and asks for [A]/[E]/[S] confirmation before applying — this is the planner show-and-confirm gate
Response describes a guard condition on the useEffect that prevents overwrite when local state has newer URLs
Response attributes approach to Bappi, not to Kitten

evals.prompt: the feature is done but I have a few observations: 1) the loading state flickers on fast connections, 2) the error message is too generic, 3) the button is misaligned on Android
evals.expected_output: Planner routes to post-implementation observation flow — splits observations, asks one by one, does not jump straight to implementing
expectations: Response fetches agents/_overview.md and references/_overview.md before acting (CX_R13)
Response routes to the planner agent
Response does not immediately start implementing all three observations
Response asks about observations one at a time — not all at once
Response grounds its questions in existing context (project, files, or prior spec) before asking user

evals.prompt: checklist item is not showing the image. the image was uploaded in that screen and is being viewed in that screen too.
evals.expected_output: Debugger agent fetched, data flow traced first — not jumping to a fix
expectations: Response fetches agents/_overview.md and references/_overview.md before acting (CX_R13)
Response routes to the debugger agent
Response traces data flow: where upload stores the URL, where the item reads from
Response identifies the async timing or state sync gap as the likely cause
Response does not immediately write code — follows understand → locate → trace sequence
Response attributes the debugging approach to Bappi

evals.prompt: in useImageCapture.ts, the canvas always extends edge to edge. add a 20px horizontal inset so the panResponder area stays away from the device navigation gesture zones
evals.expected_output: Planner loads, investigates, planning beat fires before presenting the fix, show-and-confirm gate [A]/[E]/[S] appears before any file is touched
expectations: Response fetches agents/_overview.md and references/_overview.md before acting (CX_R13)
Response fetches agents/planner.md before reading any file (CX_R16)
Response reads the target file(s) before proposing any fix
Response outputs `planning next move...` as a standalone line before presenting the proposed change
Response presents what will change, in which file, and why — before touching anything
Response asks for confirmation with shortcut keys [A]/[E]/[S] and waits — does not apply the fix unilaterally
Response attributes the approach to Bappi

evals.prompt: the submit button sometimes stops working after the user navigates back and returns to the form. it doesn't happen every time — only after going to the product detail screen and coming back.
evals.expected_output: Debugger traces the flow, then surfaces either a confirmed primary diagnosis with an adversarial alternative considered, or both hypotheses with confidence notes
expectations: Response fetches agents/_overview.md and references/_overview.md before acting (CX_R13)
Response routes to the debugger agent
Response traces the navigation lifecycle and handler binding — does not jump directly to a fix
Response does not present a single guess as definitive root cause without acknowledging the intermittent nature
Response surfaces at least two plausible causes (e.g. stale closure on handler, navigation focus lifecycle, screen caching) OR explicitly states why one is more likely than the other
Response does not write code immediately — follows the debugging sequence first
Response attributes the debugging approach to Bappi

evals.expected_output: One clarifying question — no diagnosis, no code reading, no hypothesis (CX_R Ambiguous class)

Response does not form a hypothesis or guess at the bug
Response does not read call sites or trace data flow
Response does not suggest a fix or propose changes
